note_data.title: Vocabulary
keyPoints: Vocabulary refers to the collection of words and their meanings that a person knows and uses
Context clues help determine the meaning of unfamiliar words through surrounding text
Synonyms are words with similar meanings while antonyms are words with opposite meanings
Root words, prefixes, and suffixes form the foundation of word formation in English
Homophones are words that sound alike but have different meanings and spellings
Compound words are formed by joining two or more simple words to create new meaning
Technical vocabulary includes specialized terms used in specific fields or subjects
Word families consist of words that share the same root but have different forms

definitions.definition: The collection of words and their meanings that a person knows and uses in speaking and writing
Hints or information found in surrounding text that help readers understand the meaning of unfamiliar words
Words that have the same or nearly the same meaning as another word
Words that have opposite meanings to another word
The basic word to which prefixes and suffixes are added to form new words

importantQuestions.question: Define vocabulary and give two examples.
importantQuestions.answer: Vocabulary is the collection of words and their meanings that a person knows and uses in speaking and writing. Examples: book, pen, school, Karachi.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the importance of vocabulary in language learning. How can students improve their vocabulary?
importantQuestions.answer: Vocabulary is essential for effective communication and comprehension. It helps in: 1) Better understanding of texts 2) Clear expression of ideas 3) Academic success 4) Improved reading skills. Students can improve vocabulary by: reading newspapers, using dictionary, learning word meanings, practicing synonyms and antonyms, and using new words in daily conversation.

mcqs.question: Words that sound alike but have different meanings are called:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse synonyms with antonyms
Not using context clues to understand word meanings
Memorizing words without understanding their proper usage
Confusing compound words with separate words
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs on synonyms, antonyms, and word meanings
Short questions on definitions appear frequently - memorize exact textbook definitions
Practice identifying context clues in given passages
Learn common Pakistani context examples for better marks
note_data.keyDiagram: Word Formation Tree showing root word in center with branches showing prefixes on left and suffixes on right, demonstrating how new words are formed
